集训3 · 假期复习任务卡 · Day 16 of 24

The Poem

📖 Reading

259 words · 28 target words · listen while you read

Tier 1（10词） Tier 2（6词） Tier 3（12词）

On a cloudy Saturday morning, Ivy sat by her window and finished a piece of writing about her grandmother's garden.

Her friend Zach was honest and generous with his time; he read it twice and said it was the best thing she had ever written.

At school on Monday, a boy named Cole was mean and let out a loud laugh when he saw Ivy's notebook. "That's for babies," he said. Zach told him it was wrong to make fun of someone's hard work, and Cole walked away without another word.

Ivy decided to send her writing to a local kids' magazine. She sealed it inside a letter and walked it to the post office herself.

For the next hour, Ivy could not stop thinking about it. She felt nervous and worried, rubbing her hands together every time the phone rang.

That week, she also had to handle a big science report for school. She found she liked writing much more, and she came to prefer quiet evenings with a pencil over long stretches of research.

At her grandmother's house, the smell of fresh bread always made Ivy feel calm. She would sit at the kitchen table and repeat her favorite lines to herself, just to hear how they sounded.

Two weeks later, on a bright, sunny afternoon, an envelope arrived in the mail. The editors wanted to publish her poem and print it in their next issue! Ivy dropped her backpack on the ground and cheered — good news enough to cause a celebration that lasted all evening.

🔍 Reading Comprehension

5 questions · tap to check your answer

1In "Zach told him it was wrong to make fun of someone's hard work," what does wrong mean?
B — Zach believed making fun of Ivy's work was not a fair or acceptable thing to do.
2In "She felt nervous and worried, rubbing her hands together every time the phone rang," what does nervous mean?
B — Ivy could not stop thinking about her submission and kept rubbing her hands from worry.
3In "The editors wanted to publish her poem and print it in their next issue," what does publish mean?
C — The magazine editors wanted to print Ivy's poem so readers could enjoy it.
4Why did Cole's comment not upset Ivy for long?
B — Zach spoke up for Ivy, telling Cole it was wrong to make fun of her hard work.
5What happened at the end of the story that caused a celebration?
C — An envelope arrived saying the magazine wanted to publish and print Ivy's poem, which led to a celebration.
🔁
